data.title: Qualcomm Stock (QCOM) Dips despite Looming End to UK Chip Royalty Lawsuit
data.description:
U.S. chipmaker Qualcomm's (QCOM) shares trended lower in Tuesday's pre-market session despite the planned withdrawal of the $647 million smartphone chip lawsuit filed against the company in October.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Qualcomm on Tuesday announced that the lawsuit will be pulled back. The suit had alleged that the chipmaker indirectly pushed up the cost of smartphones for consumers by forcing clients Apple (AAPL) and Samsung (SSNLF) to pay inflated royalties and license fees for critical device components.

UK consumer advocacy group Which? filed the case last year, arguing before the Competition Appeal Tribunal in London that nearly 30 million residents of the UK likely overpaid for smartphones bought in the country between October 2015 and January 2024.

However, Which?, in a new statement, said it has now reached an agreement with Qualcomm and will apply for the case to be withdrawn. The advocacy group pointed out that the lawsuit has concluded — based on evidence and argument presented at the trial — that Qualcomm did not abuse its dominant market position to coerce Apple or Samsung to agree to any licensing terms.

"Qualcomm's licensing and chipset practices did not infringe competition laws, did not result in inflated royalties, and did not lead to an increase in prices consumers paid for their mobile phones," the statement read in part, noting that the chipmaker will not make any payment to the plaintiffs in the suit.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Infleqtion (INFQ) Goes Public: A More Balanced Quantum Stock Than IonQ?
data.description:
Infleqtion Inc. (INFQ) is set to begin today its new phase as a publicly traded company on the New York Stock Exchange after closing its merger with Churchill Capital Corp X. With this move, the company becomes the first public firm focused on neutral-atom quantum tech. However, the key point for investors is not just the tech. The real question is whether Infleqtion may offer a more balanced quantum bet than peers such as IonQ (IONQ).

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Infleqtion says it designs and builds quantum computers, precision sensors, and quantum software for governments, firms, and research groups. This mix sets it apart from most public quantum names, which focus mainly on computing.

Quantum Computing Meets Precision Sensing

Most listed quantum firms are still in early stages and rely on long-term gains from quantum computers that may take years to scale. In contrast, Infleqtion also sells quantum clocks, RF systems, and inertial sensors. These tools are in use today, primarily in defense and space applications.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Should Investors Buy Palantir Stock (PLTR) after the Recent Pullback? Wall Street Weighs in
data.description:
Palantir Technologies (PLTR) stock has plunged about 27% over the past month despite the company's stellar Q4 2025 results. Broader concerns about the impact of artificial intelligence (AI) models on enterprise software companies, fears of an AI bubble, and bearish comments from Michael Burry, founder and former CEO of the now dissolved Scion Asset Management, dragged down PLTR stock. Nonetheless, Wall Street is cautiously optimistic on the data analytics company, with the average price target indicating solid upside potential from current levels.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Analysts' Views on PLTR Stock

On Monday, PhillipCapital analyst Paul Chew reiterated a Buy rating on Palantir stock but lowered his price target to $190 from $208. The 5-star analyst noted that the company's impressive Q4 2025 performance was driven by robust momentum in its U.S. Commercial segment and continued strength in the U.S. Government contract execution and new awards. Chew added that Palantir expects Q1 2026 revenue to grow about 74% year-over-year to $1.53 billion, supported by larger deal sizes and accelerating customer conversions from AIP (Artificial Intelligence Platform) bootcamps, especially in the U.S. Commercial business.

The analyst adjusted his Fiscal 2026 estimates and lowered the terminal growth rate in his model to 7.5% from 8% to reflect normalized long-term revenue growth and intensifying competition from other AI analytics providers. "We remain positive on Palantir as its Ontology moat and AIP tools drive faster enterprise AI adoption beyond defense to other industries (Commercial revenue: +73% YoY in FY26e)," said Chew.

Meanwhile, D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria reiterated a Hold rating on PLTR stock and stated that he "read all 10,000 words in Michael Burry's newsletter and found no new reason to worry about Palantir." Luria added that while he appreciates Burry and finds his criticism of the AI cycle thoughtful, he believes the Big Short investor's latest report on Palantir does not provide any new evidence or argument that would make him change his opinion. While Luria has a "positive view" of Palantir, he is neutral on the stock due to its lofty valuation.

Is PLTR a Good Stock to Buy?

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Palantir Technologies stock based on 11 Buys, six Holds, and two Sell recommendations. The average PLTR stock price target of $191.25 indicates 45.5% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: MSTR Stock Dips: Mizuho Trims Target by 20% on 'BTC Weakness'
data.description:
Mizuho Securities analyst Dan Dolev lowered his price target on Strategy (MSTR) stock due to falling Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices, which drive much of the company's valuation via its massive BTC holdings. MSTR stock fell 2.5% in pre-market trading on the news. Nonetheless, Dolev remains constructive on the shares and maintained his Buy rating because of Strategy's $2.25 billion to $2.3 billion in reserves intended to cover 2–3 years of dividends.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Dolev compared the ongoing "crypto winter" to NYC's unprecedented brutal cold snaps. This freeze, he says, has driven Strategy's Bitcoin holding value (672,500 BTC) below its roughly $76,000 average cost basis.

Dolev Updates MSTR Model Post-Q4 Results

Following Strategy's Q4FY25 results, Dolev revised his end-2027 BTC price forecast to $128,000, down from $159,000. He highlighted Strategy's massive Q4 loss that was fueled almost entirely from unrealized markdowns on its Bitcoin holdings under the new fair-value accounting standards.

Strategy reported a staggering $12.4 billion net loss ($42.93 per share) in Q4, up from $670.8 million the prior year, alongside a $17.4 billion operating loss. The loss stemmed directly from Bitcoin's sharp drop into early 2026. Management stressed these are purely accounting losses under fair-value rules, not cash flow issues.

Why Dolev Stays Bullish on MSTR

Dolev cited three prime reasons for remaining bullish on Strategy stock:

  • MSTR remains the world's largest corporate holder of Bitcoin, with 22.8% BTC yield, within its target range.
  • MSTR has sufficient cash reserves of $2.25bn–$2.3bn to survive the crypto winter.
  • Historical BTC winters prove temporary, supporting long-term upside.

What Is the Prediction for MSTR Shares?

Despite the ongoing Bitcoin weakness, analysts remain optimistic about Strategy's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, MSTR has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 11 unanimous Buys. The average Strategy price target of $359.82 implies 168.8% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, MSTR shares have dropped 59.9%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Bath & Body Works (BBWI) Stock Slides on 'Weak' Core Business
data.description:
Shares in Bath & Body Works (BBWI), the personal care and home fragrances retailer, fell about 2% early Tuesday, reversing their 5% rise the previous day. This came after investment banking giant Citi (C) downgraded BBWI, noting that the company's core business "remains weak."

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Citi Goes Bearish despite Seeing Q4 Beat

Bath & Body plans to release its earnings results for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025, which ended January 31, on March 4. Citi, like the rest of Wall Street, expects the company to beat analysts' consensus estimates.

On Wall Street, diluted earnings per share are expected to fall from $2.09 in the year-ago quarter to $1.75. Analysts also see revenue dropping 7% to $2.60 billion, down from $2.80 billion a year ago.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Nvidia Stock (NVDA) Looks Less Tasty Despite Taking AI to Noodles Centre of Japan
data.description:
Shares in semiconductor giant Nvidia (NVDA) slipped today despite striking a breakthrough AI deal in a Japanese region best known for its tasty noodles.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

First Regional AI Deal

Nvidia said that it had signed a partnership with Kagawa Prefecture in Western Japan to expand the use of AI among companies in the region and look into the development of data centers. The partnership is the first such deal struck by a local government in Japan.

Kagawa Prefecture, Japan's smallest by area and well known for udon noodles, has been promoting the region as an ideal place to work, particularly toward technology companies, due to its low risk for natural disasters and stable electricity supply.

It also hopes to improve the productivity of firms located within the prefecture by gathering companies utilizing graphics processing units developed by Nvidia, the prefectural government said.

"We take pride in Kagawa's advantageous location when it comes to the operation of GPU centers," Kagawa Gov. Toyohito Ikeda said. "We hope to expand the implementation of AI for firms in the prefecture by enlisting Nvidia's help."

Masataka Osaki, head of Nvidia's Japanese unit, said, "A range of AI catered to individual regions has been launched globally. We hope Kagawa Prefecture will become a role model across the country."

Nvidia's Global Expansion

As seen below, Nvidia's revenues are driven by the U.S. but it is trying to expand its global reach. It's already been making progress in Japan.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Tesla Stock Gains a Tailwind as Elon Musk Confirms "No Pedals or Steering Wheel" Cybercab Production Starts in April
data.description:
Tesla stock (TSLA) could be gearing up for a key tailwind following a major update from its CEO Elon Musk. On Monday, Musk took to X to repeat his aggressive timeline for the company's autonomous future. Quoting an old clip where an audience laughed at his prediction that cars would one day lose their steering wheels, Musk posted: "Cybercab, which has no pedals or steering wheel, starts production in April". This confirmation comes as the project moves into its final manufacturing phase, with the first units expected to roll off the line in less than two months.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Musks Wants to Build Vehicles that are 5 Times Faster than Before

The Cybercab represents a complete departure from the way Tesla usually makes cars. Musk explained that the company is moving toward a radical new assembly method to eventually build vehicles five times faster than before. While he expects the final production volume to be very high, he warned that the early stages in April will be "agonizingly slow" as the team gets the new system up and running. Even with rumors about safety rules possibly forcing a change, Musk is sticking to the plan that the final car will have no pedals or wheel at all. The long-term goal is to have a finished Cybercab leave the factory every 10 seconds.

Tesla Stock Reacts

Investors are currently weighing this ai">ambitious timeline against Tesla's history of meeting tough deadlines. As of Tuesday morning, Tesla stock is trading around $412.20, down about 1.26% in premarket trading. While the stock has grown about 17.5% over the last year, it recently hit a 10-week low, showing that some people are still unsure if Tesla can pull off this April goal without traditional controls. Some experts think the money from a future robotaxi software network could be a massive win, but the lack of clear legal approval for a car without a steering wheel remains a major risk for shareholders.

Musk's 10-year Prediction Comes True

The tweet that sparked this latest talk featured a 10-year-old video of Musk being mocked for his vision. Today, that vision is turning into something real, with prototypes already showing up in stores featuring road-legal lights and updated parts. By skipping the step where a human safety driver sits behind the wheel, Tesla is betting everything on the idea that its AI software can handle the road on its own from day one. If this works, the Cybercab could transform Tesla from a standard car maker into a leading self-driving taxi giant.

Is Tesla Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

On Wall Street, analysts have maintained a cautious stance on Tesla stock. According to TipRanks, TSLA stock has received a Hold consensus rating, with 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the last three months. The average 12-month price target for Tesla stock is $396.80, suggesting a downside of 5% from the current level.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: IAT Regional Banks ETF Rises as One of 2026's Best-Performing iShares ETFs
data.description:

According to on-chain data shared by an ETF tracker, IAT has delivered a 13.9% gain so far in 2026, ranking among iShares' top 10-performing ETFs this year. The strong finish positions the regional banks-focused fund alongside sector leaders in energy, semiconductors, and materials.

iShares (IAT) Joins Energy and Semiconductor Leaders

Among the top-performing iShares ETFs, IAT ranks sixth, surpassing iShares U.S. Manufacturing ETF (MADE), iShares U.S. Home Construction ETF (ITB), iShares U.S. Core High Dividend ETF (HDV), and iShares U.S. Consumer Staples ETF (IYK).

Leading the 2026 leaderboard, from first to third, are iShares U.S. Oil Equipment & Services ETF (IEZ), up 29.1% year-to-date, iShares U.S. Energy ETF (IYE), up 18.3%, and iShares U.S. Semiconductor ETF (SOXX), which posted notable gains of 15.8%, underscoring continued strength in chip-related equities.

Within this competitive field, IAT's 13.9% advance shows renewed optimism toward regional banks, a segment that faced significant volatility in prior years. Notably, analysts at TipRanks have rated this Regional Banks ETF a "Moderate Buy," with an average price target of $65.26. Meanwhile, Huntington Bancshares (HBAN), one of its holdings with the highest upside potential at 19.93%, is rated a "Strong Buy."
data.author: Joy Iyke
